mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals robust corporate hiring and underlying economic strength. This translates directly into higher aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, supporting positive equity returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown consistent growth through early 2018
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a rolling 3-year period, indicating no predictive power for 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve is actively tightening policy, evidenced by the FEDFUNDS rate rising more than 50 basis points over 6 months, the increased cost of capital and higher discount rates for future cash flows create a headwind for equity valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has been hiking rates since 2016, with the pace accelerating in 2018
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically significantly lower than in non-triggered periods (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising unemployment (UNRATE) and decelerating retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a sharp contraction in consumer health. This dual shock to labor income and spending precedes corporate earnings revisions and leads to negative equity market performance.
failure_condition: The model's hit rate (predicting negative returns when triggered) is less than 55% over a full business cycle, showing no better-than-random accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, an upside surprise in the monthly CPIAUCSL reading increases the probability of more aggressive central bank tightening. This 'bad news is bad news' dynamic leads to an immediate negative repricing of equities.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI has been trending up, crossing 2.5% YoY, focusing market attention on inflation
failure_condition: In periods where CPI YoY is above 2.5%, the correlation between monthly CPI changes and subsequent 5-day S&P 500 returns is positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy manufacturing and production sector, signaling strong business investment and output. This is a leading indicator of broad economic strength, boosting investor sentiment and near-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below a perceived natural rate (NAIRU), here proxied as 4.1%, it signals a very tight labor market. This condition sparks fears of wage-push inflation and prompts forward-looking investors to price in more aggressive Fed tightening, which is negative for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is at 4.0%, a multi-decade low, triggering late-cycle concerns
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is below 4.1%, refuting the negative signal hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) is the definitive signal of an expanding economy. This fundamental strength underpins corporate profit growth and justifies higher equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The beta of 60-day S&P 500 returns to the quarterly percentage change in GDPC1 is not statistically positive (p-value > 0.10) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a tight labor market (low UNRATE) and rising inflation (accelerating CPIAUCSL) represents the classic Phillips Curve dilemma for a central bank. This joint signal forces the Fed to prioritize inflation control via tightening, creating a strongly negative environment for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Both conditions are currently met, defining the 2018 macro regime
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return when the trigger condition is met is not negative, or is not statistically different from the average return when the condition is not met.
